description_long: This bungalow features a rectangular main structure with a pitched, terracotta-tiled gable roof and a prominent brick chimney. The exterior walls are clad in a mix of reddish-brown brick and a lighter, grayish stone section, suggesting an attached extension or garage. Large, horizontally oriented windows dominate the main facade, providing ample natural light to the interior. The roofline is simple, with two chimneys visible, one on the main section and another on the extension. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow architecture, and lacks any visible porch or veranda. The overall design is functional and modest, with a focus on horizontal lines and practical living space.
description: A single-story brick and stone bungalow with a terracotta gable roof, large horizontal windows, and two chimneys. The structure includes a main section and a smaller attached extension.
description_view_0: The bungalow is seen from above the gable end, highlighting the terracotta roof tiles and both chimneys, with the extension visible at the rear.
description_view_1: The main facade is visible, showing large horizontal windows, the main entrance area, and the brick and stone wall textures.
description_view_2: The opposite gable end is shown, emphasizing the height and brickwork of the main structure, with the roof and chimneys in view.
description_view_3: The rear or secondary facade is visible, showing the extension with its own chimney and a smaller window arrangement.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large horizontal front windows
